tluaVarargTopLevel.tlua(22,16): error TS100035: A '...' expression is only allowed inside a vararg function.


==== tluaVarargTopLevel.tlua (1 errors) ====
    declare function takesAny(...: any): void;
    declare function takesNumbers(...: number): void;
    
    // The main chunk is itself a vararg function, so top-level `...` is legal and
    // its pack is `(...any)`. This is not the 100035 error case.
    local first = ...;
    local a, b = ...;
    
    // It expands in a table constructor tail like any other pack.
    local args = {...};
    
    // And forwards into a rest parameter.
    takesAny(...);
    takesAny("x", ...);
    
    // The chunk pack is `any`, so it satisfies any element type.
    takesNumbers(...);
    
    // A nested function does not capture the chunk's pack: only the directly
    // enclosing vararg function may use `...`.
    function inner(): void
      local nope = ...;
                   ~~~
!!! error TS100035: A '...' expression is only allowed inside a vararg function.
    end
